title: triticum aestivum subsp. turgidum
intro: Triticum aestivum subsp. turgidum, commonly known as durum wheat, is a subspecies of the Triticum aestivum plant and is widely cultivated for its edible grains. The plant is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Durum wheat has been associated with several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced cholesterol levels, and enhanced immune function, due to its rich content of fiber, vitamins, and minerals. In herbal preparations, durum wheat is often used to make bread, pasta, and other baked goods, as well as being used as a supplement to support overall health and well-being.
